citations.description: Ticket criterion: '- [ ] The live store is clean after supported recovery and the blocked 75de plan review can proceed.' No matching codebase artifact found.

Ticket comment (second): 'Gerrit #875 merged... origin/main contains a09c08754. Normal rebar init ensure committed migrated tracker .gitignore at 51e5b186e; runtime markers no longer appear in status. Superseded loose PASS/signature pair moved recoverably to .scratch/fa77-b035-orphan-quarantine...' — this is operational attestation, not code.

findings.criterion: The live store is clean after supported recovery and the blocked 75de plan review can proceed.
findings.detail: This criterion concerns the live operational state of the production tracker store and the ability of a specific plan review (75de) to proceed — neither of which is visible in or verifiable from the codebase. The ticket comment provides specific identifiers (Gerrit #875, CI 29886053149, commit a09c08754, quarantine SHA256 hashes), but per the classification rules this untagged criterion is codebase-verifiable, and a ticket comment alone never satisfies a codebase-verifiable criterion. No code, test, or artifact in the repository demonstrates that the live store is clean or that the 75de plan review can proceed.
findings.remediation: Tag this criterion [operator-attested] and record a concrete attestation as a ticket comment naming: (1) a verifiable reference (Gerrit/commit/deploy id confirming the live store .gitignore migration ran), (2) the observed outcome ('git status --porcelain' output showing empty/clean), and (3) confirmation that the 75de plan review completed or was unblocked (e.g. a PASS verdict ticket id or timestamp).

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
